engineGuide.h2: Peugeot Ranch Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Peugeot Ranch across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: The workhorse 1.6 HDi powering most B9 Ranch models; reliable when maintained but EGR cooler failures drive high replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: 1.6 HDi 90 Active, 1.6 HDi 90 Allure, 1.6 HDi 112 Active, 1.6 HDi Tepee
commonFailures: EGR cooler failure (Part No. 9634714680) at 60k-90k miles
Turbo wastegate sticking causing loss of power
Injector seal leakage leading to oil dilution
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV6TED4 Peugeot Ranch engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Updated DV6 variant with revised injection mapping; improved emissions but injector sensitivity remains a concern.
compatibleVariants: 1.6 HDi 112 Active, 1.6 HDi 112 Allure, 1.6 HDi Tepee Outdoor
commonFailures: Piezo injector failure requiring full set replacement
DPF regeneration issues on short-journey use
EGR valve carbon buildup restricting flow
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV6C Peugeot Ranch engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Modern Euro 6 BlueHDi unit replacing DV6; lighter and more efficient but early EGR cooler design flaws persist.
compatibleVariants: 1.5 BlueHDi 100 Active, 1.5 BlueHDi 100 Allure, 1.5 BlueHDi 130 GT, 1.5 BlueHDi Tepee
commonFailures: EGR cooler cracking causing coolant loss
AdBlue injector clogging triggering limp mode
Timing chain tensioner wear on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV5RC Peugeot Ranch engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Robust larger diesel option for heavier Ranch loads; less common but valued for towing capability.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure between cylinders 3-4
Turbo oil feed pipe cracking
Dual-mass flywear wear causing vibration
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW10 Peugeot Ranch engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Simple mechanical diesel from first-gen Ranch; rugged and easy to repair but increasingly rare.
compatibleVariants: 1.9 D 70 Base, 1.9 D 70 LX, Ranch Escapade
commonFailures: Head gasket failure due to overheating
Mechanical injector wear causing hard starting
Glow plug relay failure in cold weather
entries.cta: Get quotes for XUD9 Peugeot Ranch engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Popular naturally aspirated petrol unit; smooth and economical but timing chain tensioner weakness defines replacement patterns.
compatibleVariants: 1.6 VTi 110 Active, 1.6 VTi 110 Allure, 1.6 VTi Tepee Outdoor
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure (OEM 06810920AA) at 70k-100k miles
Valve stem seal wear causing oil consumption
High pressure fuel pump failure on EP6 variants
entries.cta: Get quotes for TU5JP4 Peugeot Ranch engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: THP-derived variant with turbocharging; more power but increased complexity and HPFP sensitivity.
commonFailures: High pressure fuel pump (HPFP) failure
Timing chain stretch on early production units
Carbon buildup on intake valves
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP6C Peugeot Ranch engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: Modern downsized turbo petrol; efficient and responsive but timing belt-in-oil design requires strict maintenance.
compatibleVariants: 1.2 PureTech 110 Active, 1.2 PureTech 110 Allure, 1.2 PureTech 130 GT, 1.2 PureTech Tepee
commonFailures: Timing belt degradation contaminating oil system
Oil pump drive belt failure causing catastrophic damage
Carbon buildup on turbocharger vanes
entries.cta: Get quotes for EB2ADT Peugeot Ranch engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Peugeot Ranch Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Peugeot Ranch's most common replacement enquiries centre on the 1.6 HDi DV6 diesel and 1.6 VTi TU5 petrol engines. EGR cooler failures and timing chain tensioner wear drive the majority of cases. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: EGR Cooler Failure - Why Peugeot Ranch 1.6 HDi Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Exhaust gas recirculation cooler cracks internally, allowing coolant to enter the intake manifold and causing hydrostatic lock or severe engine damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and clean or replace EGR valve only; does not address cooler itself
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; cooler may still fail within 12 months

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR cooler assembly, flush intake system, reset ECU adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause if caught before engine damage; 2-3 year expectancy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt engine with upgraded EGR cooler, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution; upgraded components prevent recurrence; ideal for high-mileage Ranch

problems.recommendation: If your Ranch is under 80k miles and shows early EGR warning signs (coolant loss, white smoke), an intermediate cooler replacement may suffice. However, if the engine has suffered hydrostatic lock or misfire, a rebuilt DV6 unit with upgraded cooler is the only reliable long-term solution for a commercial vehicle.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DV6 engine prices for your Peugeot Ranch

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why Peugeot Ranch 1.6 VTi Engines Rattle
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner loses pressure or guide rails wear, allowing chain slack that causes rattling, timing drift, and potential valve-piston contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides and seals before failure occurs
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if caught early; prevents catastrophic damage; 60k+ mile expectancy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components, valve inspection, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option if valves bent or piston damage occurred; permanent fix for high-mileage units

problems.recommendation: Listen for cold-start rattling from the timing cover. If present, act immediately - a preventative chain kit is far cheaper than engine replacement. If the engine has already jumped time or won't start, a rebuilt TU5 unit is the most cost-effective path forward.
problems.cta: Get quotes for TU5JP4 Peugeot Ranch engine replacement

problems.h4: Timing Belt-in-Oil Failure - Peugeot Ranch 1.2 PureTech Risk
problems.rootCause: Timing belt runs in oil bath; degradation contaminates lubrication system, blocking oil pickup and causing bearing failure or seized engine.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, flush oil galleries, replace oil pump if accessible
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if belt debris hasn't reached bearings; strict service adherence required

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt engine with revised belt specification, upgraded oil pump, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for any EB2 showing oil pressure warnings or metallic noise; permanent solution

problems.recommendation: The EB2 design flaw is well-documented. If your Ranch is due for a belt change or shows oil pressure warnings, budget for a rebuilt unit - it's often more economical than repairing a contaminated engine.
problems.cta: Compare EB2ADT Peugeot Ranch engine prices

problems.h4: Turbo Wastegate Failure - Loss of Power on Ranch Diesels
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot Ranch 1.6 HDi / 2.0 HDi (2008-2018, DV6TED4 & DW10)
problems.rootCause: Wastegate actuator rod corrodes or linkage seizes, preventing proper boost control and triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wastegate actuator and linkage; reset turbo adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if turbo internals are sound; 2-4 year expectancy with proper maintenance

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit reconditioned turbocharger with new actuator, gaskets and lines
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses worn bearings or compressor damage; suitable for mid-life engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo, EGR delete option, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage Ranch where multiple components are worn

problems.recommendation: If loss of power is accompanied by rattling or blue smoke, the turbo is likely beyond simple repair. For Ranch vans used commercially, a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo offers the best uptime and longevity.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt diesel engines for your Peugeot Ranch

problems.h4: Injector Failure - Rough Running & Oil Dilution
problems.rootCause: Piezo injectors develop internal leaks, allowing diesel to wash into sump and dilute oil, accelerating bearing wear.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace one faulty injector, code to ECU, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Only suitable if other injectors test healthy; risk of cascade failure remains

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four injectors with calibrated units, flush oil system
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause if engine hasn't suffered bearing damage; 3-5 year expectancy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt engine with upgraded injectors, oil pump inspection, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if oil dilution has caused bearing knock or low compression

problems.recommendation: Diesel smell in oil or rough idle at tickover signals injector issues. Test all four injectors before deciding - if more than two are faulty or the engine shows bearing noise, a rebuilt unit is the safer investment.
problems.cta: Get quotes for DV6C Peugeot Ranch engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot Ranch
finalCta.paragraph: From EGR cooler cracks to timing chain wear, the Peugeot Ranch's most common failures are well understood by our specialist network. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Peugeot Ranch engine rebuilders who fit upgraded components to prevent repeat failures. Every rebuilt engine includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get your Ranch back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Peugeot Ranch Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Peugeot Ranch and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Peugeot Ranch Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Peugeot Ranch is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Peugeot Ranch 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5 BlueHDi DV5RC replaced the 1.6 HDi in later K9-generation Ranch models (2018-2024), offering improved emissions and fuel economy while retaining the platform's commercial capability. This modern diesel uses a timing chain and features an AdBlue system for Euro 6 compliance.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot Ranch 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot Ranch 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6 HDi DV6 series powered the majority of B9-generation Ranch models (2008-2018) and remains the most common replacement enquiry. Available in 90hp and 112hp outputs, these engines are robust when maintained but suffer from well-documented EGR cooler and injector issues.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot Ranch 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot Ranch 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9 D XUD9 powered first-generation Ranch models (1996-2008) and is valued for its mechanical simplicity and ease of repair. Though outdated, these engines are still sought for classic Ranch restorations and off-grid commercial use.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot Ranch 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot Ranch 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0 HDi DW10 was offered in heavier-spec Ranch models requiring extra torque for towing or payload. Less common than the 1.6 HDi but valued for its durability in commercial applications.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot Ranch 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot Ranch 1.1L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 1.1 petrol engine powered base-model first-generation Ranch vehicles. Simple and economical but underpowered for commercial use; now primarily of interest to restorers.
items.cta: Compare Peugeot Ranch 1.1L petrol engine prices

items.title: Peugeot Ranch 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2 PureTech EB2ADT represents Peugeot's modern downsized turbo petrol strategy, offering strong low-end torque and reduced emissions in K9-generation Ranch models. However, its timing belt-in-oil design requires strict adherence to service intervals.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt degradation contaminating oil system
items.cta: Compare Peugeot Ranch 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Peugeot Ranch 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4 TU3 petrol engine was a common fitment in mid-spec B9-generation Ranch models, offering a balance of affordability and adequate performance for light commercial use.
commonFailurePoints: Valve stem seal wear causing oil consumption
items.cta: Compare Peugeot Ranch 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Peugeot Ranch 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6 VTi TU5JP4 and EP6C engines powered higher-spec B9-generation Ranch models, offering smooth performance and reasonable economy. The naturally aspirated TU5 is generally more reliable than the turbocharged EP6 variant.
items.cta: Compare Peugeot Ranch 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Peugeot Ranch 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8 petrol engine was offered in early Ranch models before the 1.6 VTi became standard. Now rare but still sought for specific restoration projects. Not sure which engine size your Peugeot Ranch needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Peugeot Ranch specialists.

fuelTypes.h2: Peugeot Ranch Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Peugeot Ranch engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early 1.9 D to the latest 1.5 BlueHDi.

items.title: Peugeot Ranch Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel engines dominate the Ranch range across all generations, with the 1.6 HDi DV6 series powering most B9 models and the newer 1.5 BlueHDi DV5RC featuring in K9-generation vehicles.
items.descriptor: Diesel engines dominate the Ranch range across all generations, with the 1.6 HDi DV6 series powering most B9 models and the newer 1.5 BlueHDi DV5RC featuring in K9-generation vehicles.
items.familiesLabel: Common Peugeot Ranch Diesel Engine Families
families: DV6TED4/9HV - most common replacement engine (1.6 HDi 90/112), EGR cooler failure risk
DV6C/9HL - updated 1.6 HDi 112, piezo injector sensitivity
DV5RC - modern 1.5 BlueHDi (2018+), EGR cooler and AdBlue system concerns
DW10 - robust 2.0 HDi for heavier loads, head gasket weakness
XUD9 - simple mechanical 1.9 D (pre-2008), easy to repair but aging
foundIn: B9 generation (2008-2018) - DV6TED4, DV6C, DW10 1.6/2.0 HDi
knownFor: Strong low-end torque ideal for commercial payloads
EGR cooler failures on DV6 series between 60k-90k miles
High UK replacement demand on 1.6 HDi 90/112 variants
DPF regeneration issues on short-journey use
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot Ranch Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Peugeot Ranch Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Ranch engines span from the simple 1.1/1.4 units in early models to the modern 1.2 PureTech turbo, with the 1.6 VTi TU5 offering the best balance of reliability and performance.
items.descriptor: Petrol Ranch engines span from the simple 1.1/1.4 units in early models to the modern 1.2 PureTech turbo, with the 1.6 VTi TU5 offering the best balance of reliability and performance.
items.familiesLabel: Common Peugeot Ranch Petrol Engine Families
families: TU5JP4/NFU - reliable 1.6 VTi 110, timing chain tensioner weakness
EB2ADT - modern 1.2 PureTech turbo, timing belt-in-oil design risk
TU3JP - economical 1.4 petrol, valve stem seal wear
TU1 - basic 1.1 entry engine, now rare
foundIn: B9 generation (2008-2018) - TU3 1.4, TU5 1.6 VTi, EP6 1.6 VTi turbo
knownFor: TU5JP4 timing chain tensioner failures at 70k-100k miles
EB2ADT timing belt degradation contaminating oil system
Smooth, quiet operation compared to diesel equivalents
Lower replacement costs than diesel units on average
items.cta: Compare Peugeot Ranch Petrol engine prices

items.title: Peugeot Ranch Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: The Peugeot Ranch was not offered with hybrid or electric powertrains in the UK market; all variants use conventional petrol or diesel engines.
items.descriptor: The Peugeot Ranch was not offered with hybrid or electric powertrains in the UK market; all variants use conventional petrol or diesel engines.
items.familiesLabel: Common Peugeot Ranch Hybrid / Electrified Engine Families
importantNotes: No hybrid, plug-in hybrid or electric Ranch variants were sold in the UK
Aftermarket conversions exist but are not covered by standard replacement networks
For electric commercial vehicle needs, consider the Peugeot e-Partner (separate model)

engineYears.h2: Peugeot Ranch Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Peugeot Ranch production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original Ranch launched as a passenger-car derivative of the Partner van, offering simple petrol and diesel engines suited to light commercial use.
keyChanges: Launch of Peugeot Ranch name for Italian market (Partner-based)
Introduction of 1.9 D XUD9 diesel and 1.1/1.4/1.8 petrol options
Ranch Escapade 4x4 variant added for off-road capability
Facelift in 2002 with updated interior and safety features
knownFor: Simple mechanical engines easy to repair independently
Rust protection less robust than later generations
XUD9 diesel renowned for longevity with basic maintenance
Low parts costs but increasing scarcity for trim components
years.cta: Get quotes for 1996-2008 Peugeot Ranch replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot Ranch 1.9 D engine replacement • XUD9 head gasket fix • Ranch 1.4 petrol engine for sale

years.description: The all-new B9 platform brought modern PSA engines, improved safety and the popular Tepee passenger variant to the Ranch lineup.
keyChanges: Complete redesign on PSA B9 platform shared with Citroën Berlingo
Introduction of 1.6 HDi DV6 diesel and 1.6 VTi TU5 petrol engines
Ranch Tepee variant launched with enhanced interior space
Euro 5 emissions compliance from 2011 model year
knownFor: DV6 EGR cooler failures emerge as dominant replacement driver
TU5 timing chain tensioner issues appear at higher mileage
Improved cabin refinement and crash safety vs first generation
High parts compatibility with Partner/Berlingo simplifies sourcing
years.cta: Compare Peugeot Ranch B9 engine prices
years.ticker: Ranch 1.6 HDi EGR cooler failure • TU5 timing chain rattling noise • DV6 engine replacement cost UK

years.description: Updated B9 models received revised engine mappings, Euro 6 compliance preparations and the introduction of the 1.6 HDi 112 DV6C variant.
keyChanges: Introduction of DV6C/9HL 1.6 HDi 112 with revised injection system
EP6C turbo petrol option added for higher-spec Tepee variants
Updated infotainment and driver assistance features
Final B9 production year 2018 ahead of K9 launch
knownFor: DV6C piezo injector sensitivity increases replacement complexity
EP6 high pressure fuel pump failures drive petrol replacement enquiries
Strong residual values for well-maintained Tepee variants
EGR and DPF issues more prevalent on Euro 5/6 compliant units
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2018 Peugeot Ranch engine replacement
years.ticker: Ranch 1.6 HDi 112 injector failure • EP6 HPFP replacement cost • DV6C engine supply and fit

years.description: The all-new K9 platform introduced downsized turbo engines, advanced safety tech and stricter emissions systems to the Ranch range.
keyChanges: Launch of K9 generation with modular PSA platform
Introduction of 1.2 PureTech EB2 turbo petrol and 1.5 BlueHDi DV5 diesel
AdBlue SCR system added to diesel variants for Euro 6d compliance
Enhanced connectivity, driver aids and interior quality
knownFor: EB2 timing belt-in-oil design requires strict service adherence
DV5 EGR cooler cracking emerges as early failure pattern
Significantly improved refinement and fuel economy vs B9
Higher complexity increases specialist repair dependency
years.cta: Enquire about Peugeot Ranch K9 engine availability
years.ticker: Ranch 1.2 PureTech timing belt failure • 1.5 BlueHDi EGR cooler replacement • K9 Ranch engine compatibility

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Peugeot Ranch was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Peugeot Ranch specialists.

faq.h2: Peugeot Ranch Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Peugeot Ranch engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Peugeot Ranch engine prices typically range from £1,800 for a 1.6 VTi petrol to £3,000 for a 1.6 HDi diesel, supply only. Supply and fit adds £400-£800 depending on workshop location.
keyPoints: 1.6 VTi TU5 rebuilt: £1,800-£2,400 supply only
1.6 HDi DV6 rebuilt: £2,200-£3,000 supply only
Supply and fit labour: £400-£800 additional
Prices vary by engine code, mileage and supplier
items.cta: Compare Peugeot Ranch engine replacement prices now

items.question: What is the most reliable Peugeot Ranch engine?
items.answer: The 1.9 D XUD9 diesel is widely regarded as the most reliable Ranch engine due to its simple mechanical design, though it's now rare. Among modern units, the 1.6 VTi TU5 petrol is generally more dependable than the 1.6 HDi DV6 diesel.
keyPoints: XUD9 1.9 D: simple, robust, easy to repair independently
TU5JP4 1.6 VTi: reliable if timing chain kit replaced preventatively
DV6 1.6 HDi: strong but EGR cooler failures are common
EB2 1.2 PureTech: efficient but timing belt-in-oil requires strict servicing
items.cta: Find reliable Peugeot Ranch engine options

items.question: Why is my Peugeot Ranch 1.6 HDi losing coolant?
items.answer: Coolant loss on the Ranch 1.6 HDi is most commonly caused by EGR cooler failure, where internal cracks allow coolant to enter the intake manifold. This is a known issue on DV6TED4 and DV6C engines between 60k-90k miles.
keyPoints: EGR cooler failure is the leading cause of coolant loss
Part No. 9634714680 is the common replacement cooler
Ignoring it risks hydrostatic lock and engine damage
Upgraded coolers are available in rebuilt DV6 engines
items.cta: Get quotes for DV6 Peugeot Ranch engine replacement

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Peugeot Ranch?
items.answer: Yes, if your Ranch is in good structural condition and worth over £3,000. A rebuilt engine costs £1,800-£3,000, making replacement economically sensible compared to scrapping or buying a used van of uncertain history.
keyPoints: Ranch values typically £3,000-£8,000 depending on spec and year
Rebuilt engine cost is 30-50% of vehicle value
Rebuilt units include warranty and upgraded failure-point components
Commercial users benefit from minimal downtime with supply and fit
items.cta: Calculate if Peugeot Ranch engine replacement is worth it

items.question: What causes the rattling noise on cold start in my Ranch 1.6 VTi?
items.answer: A rattling noise on cold start in the Ranch 1.6 VTi is typically caused by timing chain tensioner failure, allowing chain slack. This is a known weakness on TU5JP4 engines and can lead to catastrophic damage if ignored.
keyPoints: Timing chain tensioner failure is common at 70k-100k miles
OEM part 06810920AA is the replacement tensioner
Immediate inspection is essential to prevent valve damage
Rebuilt TU5 engines include upgraded timing components
items.cta: Compare TU5JP4 Peugeot Ranch engine prices

items.question: Can I fit a different engine in my Peugeot Ranch?
items.answer: Engine swaps in the Ranch are complex due to platform-specific mounts, ECUs and wiring. Sticking with the original engine code is strongly recommended for reliability, emissions compliance and ease of servicing.
keyPoints: B9 and K9 platforms have unique engine mounting points
ECU and immobiliser pairing complicates non-standard swaps
Emissions testing may fail with non-original engine codes
DV6, TU5 and EB2 engines have best parts availability
items.cta: Enquire about Peugeot Ranch engine compatibility

items.question: How long does a Peugeot Ranch engine replacement take?
items.answer: A supply-only engine can be delivered in 1-3 working days from UK stock. Full supply and fit typically takes 2-4 days depending on workshop availability and ancillary parts requirements.
keyPoints: Next-day delivery available on common codes like DV6TED4
Supply and fit requires booking with a specialist garage
Additional time may be needed for EGR/DPF system calibration
Commercial users can often arrange priority fitting
items.cta: Find Peugeot Ranch engine supply and fit specialists

items.question: What warranty comes with a Peugeot Ranch replacement engine?
items.answer: All rebuilt and reconditioned Peugeot Ranch engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Terms vary by supplier, so confirm coverage details before ordering.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month warranty standard on rebuilt units
Unlimited mileage cover typical for commercial vehicles
Warranty covers parts and labour at approved fitting centres
Used engines may have limited or no warranty coverage
items.cta: Compare Peugeot Ranch engine warranty terms

items.question: How do I check my Peugeot Ranch engine code?
items.answer: Your Ranch engine code is stamped on the engine block (usually near the oil filter housing) and listed on the V5C registration document under "Engine number". You can also enter your reg on our site for instant verification.
keyPoints: Engine code format: DV6TED4, TU5JP4, EB2ADT etc.
V5C document lists engine number under section D.2
Physical stamp location varies by engine family
DVLA lookup via registration is fastest verification method
items.cta: Identify your Peugeot Ranch engine code instantly

items.question: Should I buy a used or rebuilt Peugeot Ranch engine?
items.answer: For Ranch commercial vehicles, a rebuilt engine is usually the better investment. Used engines carry unknown history and high failure risk on known weak points like EGR coolers and timing chains, whereas rebuilt units include upgraded components and warranty.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines address known failure points with upgraded parts
Used engines may have hidden wear or impending failures
Rebuilt units include 12-month warranty; used rarely do
For work vehicles, reliability and uptime outweigh initial savings
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs used Peugeot Ranch engine prices
